overview: A single Go backend service exposes the Todo REST API with a focused PATCH /todos/{id} endpoint for completion-state transitions. The service handles authentication, validation, idempotent updates, and structured error responses, and exposes /health and /metrics endpoints for observability. State is held in a thread-safe in-memory store with per-resource locking to ensure consistent concurrent updates.
requirements: PATCH /todos/{id} marks a todo completed/uncompleted via {"completed": bool} body
Operation is idempotent and updates only the completion field
Sets completedAt timestamp on transition to completed; clears it when uncompleted
Returns 200 with updated resource on success
Returns 400 for malformed JSON, wrong types, or unexpected fields
Returns 404 when todo id does not exist
Returns 415 when content type is not JSON
Returns 401 for unauthenticated and 403 for unauthorized requests
Structured error responses with code, message, and field
Log successful and failed completion attempts with context
Expose request count, latency, and error rate metrics
p95 latency under 300ms; safe concurrent updates across todos
Do not modify id, owner, or createdAt during PATCH

components.name: todo-api
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Todo API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Todo management API with PATCH support for marking todos as completed.
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:9090
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status: { type: string }
  /todos:
    get:
      summary: List todos
      security: [{ bearerAuth: [] }]
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Todo' }
    post:
      summary: Create todo
      security: [{ bearerAuth: [] }]
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [title]
              properties:
                title: { type: string }
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Todo' }
  /todos/{id}:
    parameters:
      - in: path
        name: id
        required: true
        schema: { type: string }
    get:
      summary: Get todo
      security: [{ bearerAuth: [] }]
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Todo' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
    patch:
      summary: Mark todo completion state
      security: [{ bearerAuth: [] }]
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [completed]
              additionalProperties: false
              properties:
                completed: { type: boolean }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Updated
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Todo' }
        '400': { $ref: '#/components/responses/BadRequest' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Forbidden' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '415': { $ref: '#/components/responses/UnsupportedMediaType' }
        '500': { $ref: '#/components/responses/ServerError' }
  /metrics:
    get:
      summary: Prometheus metrics
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            text/plain: {}
components:
  securitySchemes:
    bearerAuth:
      type: http
      scheme: bearer
  schemas:
    Todo:
      type: object
      properties:
        id: { type: string }
        title: { type: string }
        completed: { type: boolean }
        completedAt: { type: string, format: date-time, nullable: true }
        createdAt: { type: string, format: date-time }
        owner: { type: string }
    Error:
      type: object
      properties:
        code: { type: string }
        message: { type: string }
        field: { type: string }
  responses:
    BadRequest:
      description: Bad Request
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
    Unauthorized:
      description: Unauthorized
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
    Forbidden:
      description: Forbidden
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
    NotFound:
      description: Not Found
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
    UnsupportedMediaType:
      description: Unsupported Media Type
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
    ServerError:
      description: Internal Server Error
      content:
        application/json:
          schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
components.componentAgentInstructions:
Build a Go service using net/http on port 9090. Implement an in-memory todo store (sync.RWMutex protected map[string]Todo) seeded with a few sample todos owned by user 'user-1'.

Endpoints:
- GET /health -> {"status":"ok"}
- GET /metrics -> Prometheus text format counters: http_requests_total, http_request_duration_seconds, http_errors_total (simple manual implementation acceptable).
- GET /todos, POST /todos, GET /todos/{id}
- PATCH /todos/{id}: focus implementation here.

Auth middleware: require Authorization: Bearer <token>. Treat token as the user id. Missing/invalid -> 401 with structured error {code:'unauthenticated',message:...}. For demo accept any non-empty bearer token as that user id.

PATCH /todos/{id} behavior:
1. Verify Content-Type starts with application/json else 415 {code:'unsupported_media_type'}.
2. Parse JSON body strictly using json.Decoder with DisallowUnknownFields. Malformed JSON -> 400 {code:'invalid_json'}. Unknown fields -> 400 {code:'invalid_field', field:<name>}.
3. Body must contain 'completed' boolean. Missing or wrong type -> 400 {code:'invalid_type', field:'completed'}.
4. Look up todo by id. Not found -> 404 {code:'not_found'}.
5. If todo.owner != authenticated user -> 403 {code:'forbidden'}.
6. Acquire write lock. If completed==true: set Completed=true, CompletedAt=now (only set if not already completed; if already completed, keep existing CompletedAt for idempotency). If completed==false: set Completed=false, CompletedAt=nil. Do not modify id, title, owner, createdAt.
7. Return 200 with updated Todo JSON.

Logging: structured logs (log package fine) for each successful patch including todo id and actor. Log all failed update attempts with reason. Never leak internal errors -> 500 returns {code:'internal_error',message:'internal server error'}.

Concurrency: per-todo locking via sync.Mutex map or global RWMutex acceptable.

CORS: allow all origins for simplicity.

Dockerfile: multi-stage build, distroless or alpine final image, EXPOSE 9090.
